notes: Spec asserts: state stays ready during /stats and /help (both local-only Gemini display commands that never reach the JSONL transcript — see parser.go, which only opens a turn on a type=user text prompt). The recipe issues a warm-up turn FIRST so the per-session transcript materializes (Gemini only writes the file once a real user message lands; without it resolve_transcript can't bind). After turn_end returns to ready, the slash_window phase pins ready with duration_at_least_ms:4000 and invariants forbidding any state_transition to working/waiting — those are what would fire if /stats or /help (incorrectly) drove a model call. Unlike claudecode there is no <local-command> wrapper to filter: Gemini never persists the slash invocation at all. observations.model ('gemini-3-flash-preview') is the live-verified default boot model and is PROVISIONAL — record settles it.
source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Synchronous slash command (no LLM call)

text: Session appears in ready shortly after launch
Warm-up prompt → working (real LLM turn; materializes the transcript so subsequent slash steps run against a bound session)
Warm-up turn completes back to ready
Spec: state stays ready throughout /stats and /help — neither synchronous slash command drives a working transition
